danger_signals.visual_form: Diverse monster sprites (sword-wielding monks, dinosaurs, djinn, fish men, gargoyles, tree monsters, will-o-wisps, rock creatures, centipede women, skeleton warriors)
Large, distinctively-designed enemy sprite at level end or final area
Karnov sprite turns blue after single hit
danger_signals.meaning: One hit remaining before death; heightened danger state
Ranged threat requiring evasion or reflection

safety_signals.meaning: Upgrades Karnov's fireball capacity (up to three simultaneous projectiles)
Restores health when Karnov is in vulnerable blue state
Protective tool that reflects enemy fireballs
Screen-clearing weapon destroying all visible enemies

faction_indicators.color_identity: Flesh-toned muscular sprite; turns blue when damaged
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kusamochi Karnov (pale grey variant in Bad Dudes, representing corrupted/alternative version)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse monster designs across nine levels
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Progression through varied enemy types indicates ascending threat hierarchy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level design reflects cursed regions of Cleamina (Japanese narrative context)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: NES version replaces Wizard with three-headed dragon final boss
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Guards final treasure map piece; occupies similar themed arena to arcade version

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Diverse monster ecology across nine distinct level environments; progression through increasingly dangerous encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attack player; bosses guard progression gates; escalating difficulty through level sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Treasure map pieces accumulated after each boss defeat; visual evidence of advancement toward final objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New level environment loaded; enemy patterns reset; boss encounter triggered at level end

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red orb collection increments fireball capacity visually; blue orb restores blue-state sprite to normal coloring
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Karnov's attack capability expands; defensive vulnerability reduced; positioning changes as player gains firepower

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Story cutscenes and narrative text removed from US version; intro/ending stripped of religious context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game begins directly at level selection without narrative framing; ending shows text-only 'CONGRATULATIONS' instead of character resolution

environmental_storytelling.example: Sword-wielding monks, djinn, and magical creatures suggest mystical/cursed setting; dinosaurs and gargoyles indicate mythological realm; skeleton warriors on ostriches suggest exotic geography
Nine distinct level environments signal journey through multiple regions of Cleamina; each level completion yields map piece, creating visible accumulation toward treasure
Blue color change on Karnov indicates damage/vulnerability state without text; visual-only system communicates player health condition across language barriers
NES version's three-headed dragon replaces arcade Wizard, signaling platform-specific narrative divergence; suggests alternate magical hierarchy or corrupted transformation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal sprite coloring; full firepower capacity available; can withstand one hit
Sprite turns blue; reduced to one hit remaining; can be restored by blue orb pickup
Persistent spawning and attack patterns; projectile firing; movement toward player or environmental boundaries
Located at level terminus; requires defeat to progress; guards treasure map piece; specialized attack patterns distinguishing them from regular enemies
Stationary sprites; player contact triggers pickup; red orbs queue upgrade progression; blue orbs instantiate healing

notes: Karnov is a straightforward action-platformer with minimal narrative UI. Communication occurs primarily through sprite design, enemy variety, and item collection mechanics. Japanese version contextualizes action within religious/magical narrative (Alakatay curse, Karnov as divine agent seeking redemption), but US release strips narrative framing entirely. Visual manifestation relies on character coloring (blue damage state), enemy silhouettes, and progression markers (treasure map pieces). No faction allegiance system; world-state communication is linear and progression-based rather than dynamic or player-choice-dependent. NES modifications (blue health state, shield/spike bomb items, alternate bosses) represent mechanical storytelling divergences from arcade original.
